title: carex tristis subsp. stenocarpa
intro: Carex tristis subsp. stenocarpa, also known as the bitter sedge, is a type of sedge plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. It is native to Europe and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat digestive issues and reduce inflammation. The plant's roots and rhizomes contain compounds that have been shown to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, making it a potential remedy for a range of health issues, including gastrointestinal problems and respiratory infections. In herbal preparations, Carex tristis subsp. stenocarpa is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to take advantage of its health benefits.
